skill.name: prompt-library
skill.description: A curated prompt library with ready-to-use role, task, analysis, creative, transformation, and prompt engineering templates.
skill.summary: Use reusable prompt templates for coding, writing, analysis, creative work, and prompt refinement.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static execution, C2 keyword, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and ordinary prompt-template text in SKILL.md. The only confirmed risk factor is third-party documentation links; the skill contains no executable code, shell commands, network requests, secret access, or prompt text that tells the evaluator to skip analysis.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes third-party resource links in Markdown. They do not execute network requests, but users may leave the marketplace context if they follow them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown references, not runtime fetches or hidden callbacks. The risk is limited to user navigation and link trust.

content.value_statement: Many users waste time rewriting common prompts for coding, writing, analysis, and planning. This skill provides reusable templates and improvement techniques that make prompt work faster and more consistent.
actual_capabilities: Provides role-based prompts for developers, reviewers, writers, and architects.
Offers task prompts for debugging, refactoring, testing, API documentation, and migration.
Includes analysis prompts for complexity, performance, and security review tasks.
Supplies creative prompts for feature brainstorming and naming exercises.
Explains prompt patterns such as few-shot examples, persona framing, and structured output.
limitations: Does not run tools, inspect files, or validate answers by itself.
Requires the user to replace placeholders with accurate context and constraints.
Does not guarantee factual accuracy or security correctness in model outputs.
Provides general prompt patterns rather than domain-specific compliance guidance.

use_cases.title: Speed up code review prompts
use_cases.description: Use structured reviewer prompts to ask Claude, Codex, or Claude Code for bugs, edge cases, and maintainability concerns.

use_cases.title: Create clearer documentation requests
use_cases.description: Use technical writing templates to turn complex topics into organized explanations with examples and reader context.

use_cases.title: Plan product and creative work
use_cases.description: Use brainstorming and naming prompts to generate options, compare tradeoffs, and select practical next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a prompt template
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a ready-to-use prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a prompt from this library for [task]. Ask up to three clarifying questions, then give me a reusable prompt I can paste into Claude or Codex.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an existing prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a draft prompt that needs structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this prompt for [goal]. Preserve my intent, add missing context, define the output format, and explain the most important changes briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Create a role-based workflow prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user needs a persona and task flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a role-based prompt for [persona] working on [task]. Include responsibilities, constraints, expected output sections, and quality criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Design an advanced prompt system
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needs a reusable prompt pattern.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable prompt system for [workflow]. Include inputs, assumptions, step order, validation checks, failure modes, and output examples in plain language.

output_examples.input: User asks for a better code review prompt.
output_examples.output: A structured reviewer prompt with severity sections, edge-case checks, security review guidance, and concise reasoning expectations.

output_examples.input: User asks how to explain a complex technical topic.
output_examples.output: A documentation prompt that asks for audience level, key concepts, practical examples, and a clear final structure.

output_examples.input: User asks for feature ideas for a product.
output_examples.output: A brainstorming prompt that requests user value, implementation effort, dependencies, and a ranked shortlist.

best_practices: Replace every placeholder with concrete context before using a template.
Specify the desired output format and success criteria in the prompt.
Review generated answers for factual accuracy, security impact, and missing assumptions.
anti_patterns: Do not paste templates unchanged when the task needs specific context.
Do not ask for hidden chain-of-thought or private reasoning traces.
Do not treat generated security or legal advice as a final expert decision.

faq.question: What does this skill provide?
faq.answer: It provides reusable prompt templates for coding, writing, analysis, creative work, transformations, and prompt engineering patterns.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates are plain text and can be adapted for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and similar AI tools.

faq.question: Does the skill execute any commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown library and does not include scripts, command execution, or runtime integrations.

faq.question: Are the prompts domain specific?
faq.answer: They are general templates. Add your project details, constraints, audience, and examples for best results.

faq.question: How should I improve a template?
faq.answer: Add clear context, define the output format, include examples, and state how the answer will be evaluated.

faq.question: What should I verify after using a prompt?
faq.answer: Check facts, assumptions, security impact, completeness, and whether the answer matches your requested format.
